sources.name: Business Glossary
sources.description: In-house definitions maintained by the business.

sources.name: Free Dictionary
sources.description: Open dictionary API, no key required.

sources.name: Standards
sources.description: Curated ISO/IEC-style definitions for common business terms.

sources.name: AI Consolidated
sources.description: Definitions produced by an LLM from other sources.

terms.summary: A defined sequence of activities that transforms inputs into outputs.

definitions.text: A repeatable series of steps that produces a specific business outcome.

terms.summary: A prescribed way of carrying out a specific task within a process.

definitions.text: The step-by-step instructions for performing an activity within a process.

terms.summary: A reusable structure that guides how work is organised.

definitions.text: A set of standards, guidelines and practices that shape how a class of problems is solved.

terms.summary: The system of decision rights, accountability and oversight for an organisation.

definitions.text: The framework of rules, relationships, systems and processes by which authority is exercised and controlled.

terms.summary: Conformance with laws, regulations, standards and internal policies.

definitions.text: Adherence to relevant legal, regulatory and internal requirements applicable to the organisation.

terms.summary: The effect of uncertainty on objectives.

definitions.text: The possibility that events will occur and affect the achievement of strategic and operational objectives.

terms.summary: A mechanism that reduces the likelihood or impact of a risk.

definitions.text: Any action, policy or procedure implemented to mitigate risk and assure objectives are met.

terms.summary: A high-level statement of intent that directs decisions.

definitions.text: A formal statement of organisational intent that guides decisions to achieve desired outcomes.

terms.summary: A mandatory, measurable requirement.

definitions.text: A documented, enforceable requirement that establishes uniform criteria, methods or practices.

terms.summary: A recommended practice that is not mandatory.

definitions.text: A non-binding recommendation intended to inform judgement where standards do not apply.

terms.summary: The ability of an organisation to achieve a specific outcome.

definitions.text: A combination of people, process and technology that enables a business outcome to be produced reliably.

terms.summary: A person or group with an interest in, or affected by, the organisation.

definitions.text: An individual, group or entity that can affect, be affected by, or perceive itself to be affected by an organisation's activities.

terms.summary: A specific, measurable outcome the organisation aims to achieve.

definitions.text: A result to be achieved, typically expressed in measurable terms with a target date.

terms.summary: Key Performance Indicator — a measure of progress against an objective.

definitions.text: A quantifiable measure used to evaluate success against a key business objective.

terms.summary: A quantifiable measure used to track and assess a state or process.

definitions.text: A standard of measurement used to gauge performance, progress or quality.

terms.summary: A means of delivering value to a customer by facilitating outcomes.

definitions.text: A bounded set of capabilities offered to consumers that produces a defined business outcome.

terms.summary: A good or service offered to a market to satisfy a need.

definitions.text: Anything offered to a market for attention, acquisition, use or consumption that might satisfy a need.

terms.summary: A party that purchases or consumes the organisation's product or service.

definitions.text: An individual or organisation that receives, pays for, or is the intended beneficiary of a product or service.

terms.summary: Facts, figures and signals captured for processing or analysis.

definitions.text: Recorded facts or observations that can be stored, exchanged and processed by information systems.

terms.summary: Data that has been organised or interpreted to have meaning.

definitions.text: Data that has been contextualised, categorised or analysed so it answers a specific question.

terms.summary: A set of interacting components organised to achieve a defined purpose.

definitions.text: A coordinated set of people, processes and technology that delivers a specific capability.

terms.summary: Evidence of an activity or transaction.

definitions.text: Information created, received and maintained as evidence of an organisational activity or obligation.

terms.summary: An unplanned event that disrupts normal operations.

definitions.text: An unplanned interruption or reduction in the quality of a service or process.

terms.summary: A resource controlled by the organisation that is expected to produce value.

definitions.text: A resource with economic or operational value that is owned or controlled by the organisation.

terms.summary: A requirement the organisation must meet.

definitions.text: A legal, regulatory or contractual duty the organisation is required to fulfil.
